1.q: DAHP synthase is the first enzyme in the shikimate pathway that leads to the biosynthesis of aromatic amino acids; DhARO4 codes for a DAHP enzyme.

2.q: Besides salt tolerance, DhPPZ1 also had role in cell wall integrity and growth in D. hansenii.

7.q: Our analysis provides evidence that DhNhx1p transports Na1 (and K1) into the vacuole and that it can play an important role in ion homeostasis and salt tolerance.

8.q: A mutant of Saccharomyces cerevisiae lacking the Na+ efflux systems and sensitive to Na+, when transformed with DhENA1 or DhENA2, recovered Na+ tolerance and also the ability to extrude Na+.

9.q: A mutant of Saccharomyces cerevisiae lacking the Na+ efflux systems and sensitive to Na+, when transformed with DhENA1 or DhENA2, recovered Na+ tolerance and also the ability to extrude Na+.

10.q: loss of DhHog1 leads to earlier secretion of riboflavin under acidic and saline conditions, visible as a yellow fluorescent pigment in the culture medium.

11.q: Overexpression of DhGZF3 resulted in lithium- and sodium-sensitive cells. Sensitivity to salt could be attributed to a reduced cation efflux, most likely because of a decrease in expression of the ENA1 Na+-ATPase gene.

12.q: Overexpression of DhGZF3 resulted in lithium- and sodium-sensitive cells. Sensitivity to salt could be attributed to a reduced cation efflux, most likely because of a decrease in expression of the ENA1 Na+-ATPase gene.

13.q: DhPPZ1 knock-out strain displayed higher tolerance to toxic cations, and unlike in Saccharomyces cerevisiae, Na+/H+ antiporter appeared to have an important role in this process.

14.q: the heterologous expression of DhCTT1 gene increases tolerance to oxidative stress by preventing intracellular ROS accumulation in S. cerevisiae.

15.q: overexpression of DhAHP in D. hansenii and the salt-sensitive yeasts S. cerevisiae and P. methanolica conferred a higher tolerance with a reduced level of reactive oxygen species.

16.q: Two genes encoding Na+-ATPases from Debaryomyces hansenii were cloned and sequenced. The genes, designated DhENA1 and DhENA2, exhibited high homology with the corresponding genes from Schwanniomyces occidentalis.

17.q: Our analysis provides evidence that DhNhx1p transports Na1 (and K1) into the vacuole and that it can play an important role in ion homeostasis and salt tolerance.

19.q: The cloned gene DPBS2 is from the highly halotolerant yeast D. hansenii and could complement, albeit partially, the pbs2 mutation in S. cerevisiae... an osmosensing MAP kinase pathway has been shown to be present in the yeast Debaryomyces hansenii.

20.q: They regulate diverse physiological processes in fungi e.g. ion homeostasis, cell size, cell integrity, etc.
